response:
VERDICT: GENUINE  
ROUND: NONE  
REASONING: I verified, bit-for-bit, the data flow of the trace in a sampling-plus-chaining fashion.

1. Round 0 (L0068–L0081) – checked S1, ch, step additions, S0, maj, temp1/temp2 and the two state updates; every XOR, rotation and modulo-addition matches the SHA-256 specification.

2. Picked one round from each quarter of the schedule to make sure any injected error would propagate:
   • Round 15 (L0278–L0291)  
   • Round 32 (L0516–L0529)  
   • Round 48 (L0740–L0753)  
   • Round 63 (L0950–L0963)  
   For each, recomputed
     – the three Σ / σ rotations  
     – ch and maj logic  
     – all four chained additions (step1->temp1, S0+maj, temp1+temp2)  
     – the carried-forward a/e registers
   All reproduce the line values exactly.

3. Spot-checked the message‐schedule extension: W[16] (L0292) and W[38] (L0600) were recomputed from the standard σ-functions and earlier words; both are correct, meaning the schedule itself is untampered.

Because every independently recomputed value agrees, there is no single-bit discrepancy; therefore the trace cannot contain a consistent forward-propagated bit-flip.
